subject: Chemistry
title: E-Ink: Color Without Backlight
description: A micro-lesson that makes E-Ink: Color Without Backlight usable.

hook.text: E-readers display text using tiny charged particles that flip between black and white. No backlight means no eye strain.

buildup.text: Each pixel is a microcapsule containing black and white charged particles suspended in clear fluid.

discovery.text: Apply a positive charge: white particles rise. Negative charge: black rises. The image stays with zero power.

twist.text: Color E-Ink adds a filter layer over the capsules. But it cuts brightness by 75%, making colors muted.

climax.text: New Gallery 3 E-Ink uses colored particles instead of filters. Full-color e-readers are finally becoming vivid.

punchline.text: Charged particles in tiny capsules. A display that uses power only when it changes.

quiz.question: Why do E-Ink displays use almost no power?
options: The image stays visible without power; energy is only needed to change it
The backlight is extremely dim compared to LCD screens
The display turns off between page refreshes
